# UC-12.3.7 · Package Dependency Audit Alerts

## Description

New AGPL/GPL or typosquat package names in lockfiles—policy engine output beyond CVE severity.

## Value

New AGPL/GPL or typosquat package names in lockfiles—policy engine output beyond CVE severity.

## Implementation

Map policies to Splunk alerts. Weekly license review for new copyleft in commercial products.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: FOSSA, `npm audit` JSON, OSV.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Policy violation events `license_policy`, `dependency_policy`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map policies to Splunk alerts. Weekly license review for new copyleft in commercial products.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="sca:policy"
| where policy_result="violation" OR risk="blocked"
| stats count by project, package_name, policy_name
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Package Dependency Audit Alerts** — New AGPL/GPL or typosquat package names in lockfiles—policy engine output beyond CVE severity.

Documented **Data sources**: Policy violation events `license_policy`, `dependency_policy`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): FOSSA, `npm audit` JSON, OSV. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: sca:policy. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="sca:policy". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where policy_result="violation" OR risk="blocked"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by project, package_name, policy_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the vendor console or native job history for that tool. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (violations), Bar chart (by policy), Timeline.

## SPL

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="sca:policy"
| where policy_result="violation" OR risk="blocked"
| stats count by project, package_name, policy_name
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (violations), Bar chart (by policy), Timeline.
